repository.head: 2fb5a8a is the source commit used for the latest standard NSIS candidate; the final documentation commit contains this memory
repository.remote_head: the online flash algorithm-source implementation and final handoff commits are pushed together on feature/online-flash-streaming
repository.working_tree: clean after the final documentation commit; generated installers, screenshots, logs, caches, dependencies, and build outputs removed

mission.product: MKLink 专用嵌入式上位机
mission.online_flash: 仅支持 MKLink 暴露的 CMSIS-DAP；支持 BIN 与 HEX；优先使用许可明确的精简内置算法，也支持用户导入 Pack、按器件添加自制 FLM，以及可选联网下载 Pack。
mission.offline_flash: 脱机烧录是与在线烧录并列的顶级页面；支持有序多固件、多 FLM、BIN 基地址、HEX 文件内地址、自动次数、IDCODE 超时、SWD 速率、脚本预览和 MKLink U 盘部署。
mission.streaming: SystemView、VOFA、RTT 和 SuperWatch 以经过验证的稳定负载采集，目标尽量达到或超过 10 kSamples/s；仅 RTT 建立了实测过载边界；可见曲线统一限制为 30 FPS。

current_session.active_plan: none; online flash 10 MHz, bundled algorithms, local Pack import, custom FLM, frozen Pack worker hardening, standard NSIS packaging, and physical BOOT/RGB_LCD qualification are complete
current_session.current_task: The 2fb5a8a v0.1.0-rc.2 standard NSIS candidate is installed and hardware-qualified. Expand the curated builtin algorithm set only after redistribution-license review, then validate the candidate on a second clean Windows machine.
current_session.latest_commit: 2fb5a8a fix: import CMSIS Pack descriptors from archives
current_session.latest_installer_bundle: complete; source commit 2fb5a8a produced only the standard NSIS candidate release/20260719-211720/Mklink-AI-Probe_0.1.0-rc.2_x64-standard_2fb5a8a-setup.exe plus SHA256SUMS.txt. SHA-256 is def00101776f9a0fe35f420e5d14ed6e14e04f8c4a520b9afc0e9c2470fcec62. No MSI or WebView2-offline package was generated. Silent overwrite install, restricted-PATH bundled-sidecar launch, visible build identity, builtin target discovery, frozen online Pack install, local Pack import, BOOT internal Flash HIL, RGB_LCD external QSPI FLM HIL, normal shutdown, zero Python processes, and API/CDP port cleanup passed.
current_session.online_flash_algorithm_sources_implement: complete; online SWD offers and enforces a 10 MHz maximum. The sidecar bundles six license-reviewed slim STM32 DFPs containing only PDSC, referenced FLM, and license files: 1142 exact targets in 4.56 MiB. User-installed or downloaded Packs override duplicate builtin records, the GUI exposes local .pack import, and target-scoped custom FLM records persist for additional external Flash regions. Frozen Pack operations use internal worker and process-guard entrypoints with parent-death Jobs, HTTPS provenance validation, atomic commit, and privacy-safe API responses. Real installed WebView2 HIL programmed and verified both internal Flash and external QSPI Flash.
current_session.superwatch_stable_viewport_implementatio: complete; the dynamic cur/min/max/avg footer and all responsive footer CSS are removed, so changing digit widths cannot resize the chart. The shared Y axis starts in Auto, captures absolute manualMin/manualMax on Y interaction, anchors wheel zoom to the pointer value, keeps later outliers from moving a manual viewport, and restores Auto on double-click. Plain plot left-drag uses independent 5 px X/Y activation, pans Y by absolute range, pans X only when zoomed using visibleRange divided by availableRange, preserves cursor/trigger/Alt/middle/Space/VOFA/probe-click priority, and persists manual state without leaking it across project loads.
current_session.superwatch_throughput_implementation: complete; RTT transmit retains the input after successful sends and permits an empty body when an explicit line ending is selected. SuperWatch desktop buffers are at least 50000 points per channel, the supported interval range is 10 us to 60 s, and Canvas renders Worker-generated pixel envelopes instead of scanning the full ring. StreamHub late subscription atomically replays and protects SuperWatch metadata without lock inversion or producer blocking. The SuperWatch running boundary reconnects the binary stream after reset so the new Worker session receives metadata before samples. The strict packaged gate tracks the actual target Worker by transferred ArrayBuffer identity, counts accepted frames across resets, rejects every Worker error, and keys diagnostics by connection generation plus frame ticket.
current_session.rtt_packaged_repair: complete; the installed Tauri frontend now receives an explicit http://127.0.0.1:8765 control-plane origin instead of resolving /api to tauri.localhost index.html. RTT View always renders the text data panel, uses the user-proven dynamic 1024-byte scan, shows a deduplicated startup state, waits for a real control block, retries stop-pending cleanup, restores an already-running session on re-entry, and enables transmit only for active channel 0. Device.rtt_start treats matching target RAM DownBuffer descriptors as authoritative over corrupt nonempty probe output in both dynamic and exact modes. The lower-right footer shows v0.1.0-rc.2 plus the source commit.
current_session.desktop_configuration_implementation: complete; the separate configuration and connection tabs are replaced by one configuration workspace with Local Device, File Sources, Remote Connection, and Start Service sections. Project overview, project directory/status, recent projects, MCU guidance, RTT advanced settings, and duplicated status blocks are removed. Users provide one AXF/ELF/OUT symbol path and an independent MAP path; invalid symbol paths do not block device connection. Tauri native file dialogs are registered and permissioned.
current_session.rtt_bidirectional_implementation: complete; RTT View owns the editable address, auto-search fills it from AXF/ELF first, MAP second, and legacy project discovery last, and the user explicitly clicks Start. The transmit bar supports Abc/Hex toggle, Enter and button send, bounded history, clear, and no/\r/\n/\r\n endings. Backend writes are manager-owned, serialized outside the event loop, channel-aware, and protected by dashboard start transaction locking. V4.3.4 exact-mode startup is synchronized and retried with a four-byte bounded scan while target control-block DownBuffer descriptors remain authoritative.
current_session.offline_first_load_retest: complete; three valid installed-application cold starts opened Offline Flash through real Microsoft Edge/Playwright against the bundled sidecar and physical V4.3.4 probe. The initial target request returned HTTP 200 in the captured rounds, one matching target appeared without manual retry, model detection completed, and no online flash operation failed message appeared.
current_session.superwatch_interval_implementation: complete; SuperWatch defaults to 0.001 seconds across desktop, CLI, Python, manager, and legacy viewers. The backend dynamically stops and reissues cmd.dump_memory when the interval changes. The desktop input now preserves a user draft across status polling and mouse-focus transitions by tracking dirty and pending states, then displays the normalized server value after the request completes.
current_session.online_flash_discovery_implementation: complete; the packaged sidecar includes pyOCD and cmsis_pack_manager runtime data, the API maps enumeration failures to an actionable privacy-safe error, and page-entry discovery retries both empty results and transient cold-start failures before selecting the first matching MicroKeen probe. Selecting HEX starts inspection automatically. Selecting BIN requires an explicit valid base address and then starts inspection automatically. The manual inspect button was removed.
current_session.superwatch_symbol_workspace_implementati: complete; AXF symbols load immediately into a shared runtime-safe catalog, SuperWatch offers searchable selection and typed inline writes, and reparse preserves selected names. The redundant right Watch panel is removed, so the waveform fills the right workspace. Variable checkboxes control acquisition while adjacent eye controls independently hide or restore curves without stopping sampling or current-value updates. The waveform has a shared numeric Y axis with wheel zoom, drag pan, and double-click reset, plus the existing independent X-axis navigation. Typed writes stop continuous dump acquisition, use flush_memory, verify with command-mode readback, and restore the prior running or paused state. Reconnecting rebinds the existing runtime to the current Device instead of retaining a closed Device object.
current_session.desktop_usability_implementation: complete; Computer Use exercised connection, RTT, RTOS Trace, memory, symbols, SuperWatch, navigation, pause/resume/stop, and resource recovery before its capture channel failed. Real Edge/Playwright continued visual testing. SuperWatch stop no longer opens an English blocking confirmation, stop HTTP failures are visible, invalid symbols use a persistent Chinese inline error instead of alert(), and offline structured PROBE_BUSY details identify the owning dashboard and recovery action. A rebuilt unsigned NSIS installer was installed and the installed application passed the affected flows with Computer Use.
current_session.rtt_dashboard_implementation: complete; RTT reads now deliver at 50 Hz with a 10 ms bridge polling quantum, partial numeric batches flush every read cycle, and the virtual log coalesces near 30 FPS instead of 10 FPS. Numeric channel layouts require two consecutive matching rows so attaching mid-line cannot lock a truncated name. The demonstration firmware emits changing temp/speed values. Real Edge HIL confirmed named curves, 23.8 Canvas FPS, about 25 ms log update intervals, pause/resume behavior, and zero transport/backend loss.
current_session.offline_download_implementation: complete; top-level GUI, structured API/core, transactional U-disk deployment, model-specific filenames, trigger resource coordination, transient version retry, reuse of an already connected Device bridge in installed runtime, automated regression, and V4.3.4 dual-HEX HIL all pass

current_session.task_11_implementation: complete; final MSI/NSIS bundles rebuilt from 56f1df6, final NSIS installed-GUI verify and VERIFY_FAIL mismatch-address HIL pass, both restricted-PATH installer lifecycles pass, and Pack/user caches are preserved
current_session.task_12_implementation: complete; 21 local release payloads and 18 sanitized JSON artifacts validate, SHA256SUMS is name-sorted, and final specification and quality reviews are APPROVED with no remaining findings

current_session.release_qualification: Complete. The reviewed release commit is 1c7c5aac3f49f95d4195a0f07eed51bdaf6dcde6, the annotated tag and manifest identify that commit, and the GitHub prerelease is public and verified.
current_session.pause_requested: Resumed by user; the packaging pause checkpoint is closed.
current_session.stop_rule: Continue autonomously through implementation, verification, standard NSIS packaging when needed, memory, commit, and push. Do not build MSI or WebView2-offline packages unless the user explicitly requests them. Stop only for irreplaceable credentials, required manual hardware action, or an unauthorized irreversible external operation.

Real Edge visible/pause/resume gates passed with <=30 FPS and zero measured loss; hidden state was not established. milestones.name: Offline download configurator
milestones.tests: Python 634 passed in 37.69s; Node release harness 51 passed; GUI 19 files / 246 tests passed in 18.16s; Vite 144 modules; Rust 6 passed and cargo check completed
milestones.evidence: V4.3.4 detected through cmd.get_version(); offline_download.py deployed with two ordered HEX files; load.offline() completed both downloads; independent contiguous Flash readback matched 19,296 bootloader bytes and 113,728 application bytes
milestones.review: full automated regression, diff validation, privacy scan, and real hardware closed loop completed
milestones.notes: V2/V3 always generate python/offline_download.py. V4 accepts a safe custom .py filename, requires screen selection confirmation before GUI trigger, and uses offline_download.py for unattended HIL. No whole-chip erase command is generated. Installed runtime detection, automatic preview/deploy, and trigger reuse the connected Device bridge instead of opening the CDC port twice.

milestones.name: RTT dashboard runtime-state repair
milestones.tests: Python 635 passed in 27.33s; Node release harness 51 passed; GUI 19 files / 249 tests passed in 17.38s; Vite 144 modules; Rust 6 passed; focused RTT Worker/View 72 passed
milestones.evidence: Rebuilt installed NSIS restored the project and connected with AXF symbols. Initial HIL received ordered RTT raw and four-channel waveform payloads with zero drops. After the rendering correction, real Edge visible-window HIL processed 93 data frames and 20,093 items in 10 seconds with 37 Canvas clears and 148 strokes, zero frontend sequence errors, zero transport/backend drops, pause with zero draws, resume with 20 clears and 80 strokes, complete resource cleanup, and RAM fixture dearm.
milestones.review: focused RED/GREEN regression, full automated regression, production build, bundle install, sanitized HIL, diff validation, and generated-artifact cleanup completed
milestones.notes: RTT start now returns a success boolean to the component, so a failed control-plane start cannot create an orphan binary WebSocket. The manager detects Device ERROR, stops, releases its API lease through the failure callback, exposes status.error, and the RTT toolbar stops the binary client and displays the runtime error. The numeric Canvas remains hidden until numeric RTT data arrives. The Worker includes bufferStartMs/bufferEndMs in numeric batches and RTT View requests envelopes over that finite range, preventing all samples from collapsing into one pixel and one invisible point per constant channel. Unsigned rebuilt NSIS SHA-256 F7C84453687ABCEF669CAA36EAEC75CA5A42ED12124119FCAF9433312D9C709E; MSI SHA-256 134B3B426A3F6FFA19440FD755BF196A3664F9AA18BC40083E11C78301307676.

milestones.name: RTT named waveform and smooth refresh
milestones.tests: Python 636 passed in 27.32s; GUI 19 files / 249 tests passed in 16.97s; Vite 144 modules; Rust 6 passed; focused RTT Python 25 and GUI 13 passed; Keil App build 0 errors and 0 warnings
milestones.evidence: Bootloader-preserving App HEX covered 0x08005000 through 0x08020C97 and passed Flash readback verification without whole-chip erase. Installed-runtime HIL recognized speed,temp after attaching to an already-running stream, delivered more than 3,000 parsed points in three seconds with zero drops, and rendered two changing named curves. A five-second Edge gate measured 119 Canvas clears, 238 strokes, 23.8 FPS, about 25 ms log update intervals, zero draws while paused, 29 draws in 1.2 seconds after resume, and clean stop.
milestones.review: root-cause analysis, focused and full regression, production build, NSIS/MSI bundle, installed sidecar smoke, real Edge Worker/Canvas gate, diff validation, privacy-safe memory update, and generated-artifact cleanup completed
milestones.notes: RTT delivery uses 50 Hz read windows and flushes incomplete numeric batches each cycle. The bridge polling quantum is 10 ms. The virtual log flush interval is 33 ms. Two consecutive matching numeric layouts are required before channel lock, preventing a mid-line first chunk such as peed=90 from replacing speed. Unsigned rebuilt NSIS SHA-256 E6D8B4A695FDEE8C56F331D6055A6E0B6D43C2B8605216934EE848EEE34E30EA; MSI SHA-256 CA0234FABED078F7242CE1AE3F0D060C76A38D416B4F51F949813E2FD013A5E9.

milestones.name: Installed GUI ordinary-user experience recovery
milestones.tests: GUI 20 files / 252 tests passed; Vite transformed 144 modules; focused RED/GREEN tests cover structured offline conflicts, nonblocking invalid-symbol feedback, and direct stop behavior
milestones.evidence: Computer Use completed real installed-app connection, RTT, RTOS Trace, memory, symbol, and SuperWatch interactions. Edge/Playwright reproduced the resource-conflict chain and verified the fixes. A fresh unsigned NSIS bundle installed with exit 0; the installed application restored the project, connected to the real target, displayed the invalid-symbol error inline without a dialog, stopped SuperWatch directly, released the bridge, and detected V4.3.4 on the offline page.
milestones.review: root-cause tracing, focused RED/GREEN tests, full GUI regression, production build, NSIS build/install, installed Computer Use retest, privacy cleanup, and diff validation completed
milestones.notes: The original SuperWatch stop confirmation caused Computer Use click timeout and retained the bridge lease. Offline detect then received a structured PROBE_BUSY response, but the frontend discarded the object and showed only Conflict. Both ends of that user-visible chain are fixed.

milestones.name: Installed online-flash MicroKeen discovery and firmware auto-inspection
milestones.tests: GUI 20 files / 255 tests passed; online-flash GUI 48 passed; online-flash Python 96 passed; Rust 6 passed; Vite transformed 144 modules
milestones.evidence: Computer Use cold-started the rebuilt unsigned NSIS installation, entered Online Flash from another page, and observed MicroKeenV4 CMSIS-DAP automatically discovered and selected without pressing Refresh. A minimal App-address HEX was automatically inspected. A BIN remained blocked until an explicit App base address was entered, then automatically inspected. No erase, program, or whole-chip operation was started.
milestones.review: root-cause tracing, RED/GREEN transient-enumeration coverage, full regression, production and NSIS-only build, silent overwrite install, installed Computer Use retest, privacy cleanup, and diff validation completed
milestones.notes: All CMSIS-DAP descriptions containing MicroKeen remain allowed, covering V2, V3, V4, and future MicroKeen variants while unique_id distinguishes devices. The original installed sidecar omitted pyOCD parser data and cmsis_pack_manager native runtime files. After packaging those dependencies, page-entry discovery still stopped on the first cold-start 500 because only empty arrays were retried; discovery now retries bounded transient errors as well.

milestones.name: SuperWatch symbol workspace and reconnect-safe typed writes
milestones.tests: Python 659 passed; GUI 24 files / 273 tests passed; Rust 6 passed; focused SuperWatch transaction regression 4 passed; Vite production build and MSI/NSIS bundles completed
milestones.evidence: Computer Use verified the installed application after an explicit disconnect/reconnect cycle. The stable init_ok variable displayed 'verified: 0' after the typed write, no Failed to fetch error appeared, continuous dump acquisition resumed immediately, and backend read cycles increased by 21 in two seconds with zero read errors. Pause, resume, stop, buffer reset, symbol reparse preservation, X/Y zoom, and drag pan also passed. Online Flash automatically discovered the MicroKeen probe, automatically inspected the App HEX, programmed only the embedded App range, and completed connect, erase, program, verify, reset, and disconnect without whole-chip erase. The matching BIN required an explicit App base and then inspected automatically.
milestones.review: ordinary-user Computer Use exploration, root-cause tracing, focused RED/GREEN tests, two rebuilt installer overwrite cycles, real target retest, full automated regression, privacy scan, generated-artifact cleanup, and diff validation completed
milestones.notes: The first failure had two layers. SuperWatchRuntime survived reconnect but prepare() returned before rebinding the current Device, so writes used a closed Device while polling used the new Device. After rebinding, one-shot dump_memory readback could leave firmware unable to restart continuous dump; command-mode read_memory verification avoids that stream transition and restores acquisition reliably.

milestones.name: SuperWatch waveform visibility and shared-axis workspace
milestones.tests: Python 659 passed in 37.95s; GUI 24 files / 279 tests passed; focused SuperWatch tests 55 passed; WaveformViewer 48 passed in isolation; Vite transformed 1906 modules; Rust 6 passed and cargo check completed
milestones.evidence: Computer Use verified the installed application with two changing float variables. The redundant right Watch table was absent and the waveform filled the right workspace. Hiding one selected variable through its eye control removed only that curve while its displayed value continued changing; restoring the eye brought the curve back. Six shared Y-axis tick values were visible. Y wheel zoom changed the range, drag shifted its center, and double-click restored the default range. Pause froze the visible point count while the transport buffer continued growing, resume advanced the curve to the current buffer, stop cleared the backend buffer to zero, Alt+F4 closed the application, and the sidecar, port, and development server resources were released.
milestones.review: design refinement, implementation plan, focused component regression, full Python/GUI/Rust regression, production build, installed Computer Use HIL, privacy-safe memory update, diff validation, and generated-artifact cleanup completed
milestones.notes: The first full GUI run shared the host with Python and Rust and exceeded the synthetic VOFA peak-heap threshold by about 15 MiB while all functional assertions passed. The complete WaveformViewer file and the full GUI suite both passed when rerun without competing toolchains, identifying parallel host memory pressure rather than a stable product regression.

milestones.name: Standalone Windows installer candidates for 04c1ca7
milestones.tests: Tauri built standard and WebView2-offline MSI/NSIS bundles; standard NSIS and offline NSIS silent overwrite installs exited 0; restricted-PATH API health and MicroKeen discovery passed with zero Python processes; MSI administrative extraction contained the main executable and 62,076,496-byte sidecar; normal shutdown released all Mklink processes and port 8765
milestones.evidence: The standard installers are approximately 61-62 MiB and use Tauri's WebView2 download bootstrapper only when the target lacks WebView2. The offline installers are approximately 257-258 MiB and embed the Microsoft WebView2 offline runtime. Both installed runs started the bundled PyInstaller sidecar from the application directory while PATH contained only Windows system directories. The health API returned ok, one MicroKeen CMSIS-DAP was enumerated, no python.exe or pythonw.exe process existed, and shutdown released the sidecar and listener.
milestones.review: forced sidecar rebuild with mklink, pyOCD metadata/plugins, cmsis_pack_manager and HID collection; standard and offline Tauri bundles; restricted-environment install/runtime checks; MSI content extraction; SHA-256 generation; external release-directory publication; generated-artifact cleanup
milestones.notes: The packages are intentionally not committed. The release directory is excluded by the main repository's local Git exclude. All current installers remain unsigned and may show an unknown-publisher warning.

milestones.name: SuperWatch 1 ms default and live interval editing
milestones.tests: Focused WaveformViewer 49 passed; focused SuperWatch/stream Python 64 passed; GUI 24 files / 280 tests passed twice; Python 660 passed; Vite transformed 1906 modules
milestones.evidence: The standard NSIS overwrite install exited 0. Computer Use established the initial 0.001 second default and reproduced status polling overwriting an in-progress edit. After Computer Use became unavailable following a pause, real Microsoft Edge with Playwright used the production frontend and the installed bundled sidecar against the physical target. The 0.002 draft survived more than two seconds of polling, Apply changed both UI and backend to 0.002 while state remained running, read cycles and points continued advancing, pause kept backend acquisition active, resume restored rendering, stop reached stopped with zero read errors, the browser client count returned to zero, and all test processes and ports were released.
milestones.review: ordinary-user reproduction, focused regression, two real-input timing iterations, full GUI/Python regression, production build, standard NSIS build/install, real Edge hardware retest, privacy cleanup, and generated-artifact cleanup completed
milestones.notes: The first implementation already supported dynamic cmd.dump_memory restart, but status polling rewrote the number input before the user could submit. A focus-only guard passed synthetic click tests but failed with real mouse timing because blur could run before click. The final dirty/pending model is independent of focus event ordering. Completed SuperWatch plans were removed and the active Tauri builder skill was rewritten and synchronized.

milestones.name: Offline Flash first-load target-search retest
milestones.tests: Three installed-application cold-start rounds with the standard 2b5ddd7 NSIS candidate, real Microsoft Edge/Playwright, bundled sidecar, and physical V4.3.4 probe
milestones.evidence: Each valid round entered Offline Flash immediately after port 8765 became available. Target search produced one matching result without manual retry and no alert. The two instrumented rounds captured the first /api/online-flash/targets request returning HTTP 200; probe model detection completed as V4.3.4.
milestones.review: No product-code change was required; the previously observed transient could not be reproduced after the existing cold-start and packaged-runtime repairs.
milestones.notes: The original one-off online flash operation failed observation is closed by repeated installed-runtime retest. No screenshots, raw logs, probe identifiers, COM ports, or local hardware paths are retained.

milestones.name: Desktop configuration workspace and bidirectional RTT
milestones.tests: Python 686 passed; GUI 30 files / 324 tests passed; Vite transformed 1908 modules; Rust 6 passed and cargo check completed; focused backend review regressions 113 passed
milestones.evidence: Real Microsoft Edge/Playwright at 1200x800 and 800x600 verified the four-section configuration workspace, removed duplicate project/MCU/status content, AXF/ELF and MAP validation, retained remote/server controls, RTT automatic address fill, explicit Start, Enter send, button send, history, mode toggle, and non-overlapping layout. Physical V4.3.4 HIL verified exact-mode recovery to a four-byte bounded scan, three target DownBuffer descriptors with active channel 0, repeated start/stop, UTF-8, 00ff, no ending, carriage return, line feed, and combined carriage-return/line-feed byte-for-byte echo. The installed standard NSIS ran under a PATH containing only Windows system directories, used mklink-ai-probe.exe plus bundled mklink-sidecar.exe with zero Python processes, passed MAP address detection and RTT write on hardware, then exited normally and released port 8765.
milestones.review: Independent frontend and backend reviews completed. Search/start and pending-send UI races, dashboard start callback generation replacement, event-loop blocking, current-channel validation, and configured-address compatibility were fixed with RED/GREEN tests. Final backend re-review approved with no Critical or Important findings.
milestones.notes: The standard NSIS is unsigned. AXF/ELF address diagnosis still depends on external arm-none-eabi-readelf; the restricted installed-runtime test intentionally omitted it, so MAP diagnosis was used and passed. No installer, checksum, screenshot, raw log, firmware, Pack, probe identifier, COM port, user name, credential, or local hardware path is committed.

milestones.name: RTT transmit retention and SuperWatch 10 us throughput

milestones.evidence: Installed Tauri WebView2/CDP at a 10 us request completed a strict 600.737-second physical SuperWatch gate. All 457776 WebSocket frames matched 457776 target Worker accepted frames, final sequence matched, Worker errors were zero, and frontend transport, frontend backend, backend queue, parser, target packet, and target overflow drops were all zero. Two float channels produced 14628202 sample rows at 24350.43 samples/s/channel. Canvas rendered 13843 frames and 193802 strokes at 23.04 FPS, paused with zero draws while acquisition continued, resumed drawing, drained fully, stopped, released the dashboard lease, and closed with zero Mklink processes, zero API/CDP listeners, and zero Python processes. The installed footer showed v0.1.0-rc.2 plus a9f1b6b.

Final reviews reported no Critical or Important findings.
milestones.notes: The 10 us setting is the fastest supported request and the qualified two-channel stable rate, not a proven absolute hardware maximum. Only the standard unsigned NSIS was generated. No MSI, WebView2-offline bundle, raw log, screenshot, firmware, Pack, probe identifier, COM port, user name, credential, or local hardware path is committed.

verification.area: Online flash algorithm sources and installed hardware qualification
verification.result: Python 746, GUI 348, and Rust 6 tests passed; Vite transformed 1908 modules and cargo check completed. The 2fb5a8a installed standard NSIS exposed the matching build identity, a visible 10 MHz SWD option, local Pack import, target-scoped custom FLM, and 1142 exact builtin targets from a 4.56 MiB slim bundle. A previously uninstalled DFP completed the frozen HTTPS Pack install path and a real local Pack archive imported successfully; both privacy-safe responses omitted local paths. Real Tauri WebView2/Playwright HIL programmed, readback-verified, reset, and disconnected a BOOT internal-Flash HEX and an RGB_LCD external-QSPI HEX using the persisted custom FLM, with complete ordered job states, zero console errors, no active job or resource owner, normal application close, zero Python processes, and released API/CDP ports. Only standard NSIS was generated; SHA-256 is def00101776f9a0fe35f420e5d14ed6e14e04f8c4a520b9afc0e9c2470fcec62.

decisions: Only MKLink-exposed CMSIS-DAP is supported by the online flash UI.
BIN requires an explicit base address; HEX uses embedded mappings.

Online flash algorithm acquisition is offline-first: license-reviewed slim builtin DFPs are preferred, users can import local .pack archives, users can attach target-scoped custom FLM for additional Flash regions, and HTTPS Pack download remains optional.

User-installed or downloaded Pack records override duplicate builtin bundle records. Custom FLM may add non-overlapping external Flash regions but cannot silently replace an existing Pack algorithm for the same address range.
Builtin algorithm bundles are generated deterministically from an explicit allowlist and contain only PDSC, referenced FLM, and required license files. Pack or FLM binaries remain outside Git.
High-rate data uses a versioned binary WebSocket data plane; REST/SSE remains for control and legacy low-rate paths.
Acquisition must never wait for a browser; each client has a bounded queue that drops the oldest batch with explicit telemetry.
Rendering is decoupled from acquisition and capped at 30 FPS with min/max pixel-envelope decimation.

Ordinary online-flash program is enforced at both GUI and API boundaries: the inspected image is target-bound, reliable FLM coverage is recomputed, and erase sectors must exactly equal image-covered sectors; whole-chip erase remains a separate explicit confirmation.
Offline probe model selection is derived from cmd.get_version(). V2/V3 deploy python/offline_download.py; V4 may deploy a safe custom .py name selected on the probe screen.
Offline scripts preserve firmware order, use embedded HEX addresses, require BIN base addresses, and never generate a whole-chip erase operation.
The GUI trigger is disabled until the current configuration is deployed; V4 custom scripts require explicit confirmation that the same script is selected on the probe screen.
RTT View always renders the text data panel, while the numeric Canvas appears only when numeric channels are available.

RTT binary transport starts only after a successful control-plane start. A runtime Device ERROR is terminal, releases the dashboard lease, is exposed as status.error, and stops the frontend binary client.

RTT waveform envelopes use the Worker's finite buffered timestamp range. Requesting 0..Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER is forbidden because it collapses real-time samples into one pixel and makes constant channels invisible.
RTT dashboard delivery uses 50 Hz read windows, flushes partial raw and numeric batches each cycle, and keeps Canvas rendering capped by the shared 30 FPS scheduler.
RTT numeric channel names are locked only after two consecutive rows expose the same layout; the first stream chunk may begin in the middle of a line.
SuperWatch typed writes serialize against acquisition: stop continuous dump, write through flush_memory, verify with command-mode read_memory, then restore the prior running or paused state.
SuperWatch prepare always rebinds the current Device on reconnect even when the symbol runtime and selected watch list are preserved.

SuperWatch variable checkboxes control acquisition while adjacent eye controls affect rendering only; hidden curves keep sampling and updating their current values. The desktop waveform has no dynamic statistics footer. Its shared Y axis starts in Auto, uses an absolute manual range after Y interaction, anchors wheel zoom to the pointer, supports plot Y/XY panning with independent activation thresholds, and returns to Auto on double-click.

Every future Windows candidate is copied to the main repository's external release directory with the source commit in the filename and a SHA-256 checksum list. Generate the standard NSIS installer by default. Do not generate MSI or WebView2-offline installers unless the user explicitly requests them. Installers and checksums are never committed.
Subagent workflow is implementer, spec review, then quality review; review fixes receive new commits and are re-reviewed.

The desktop configuration surface is one workspace with Local Device, File Sources, Remote Connection, and Start Service sections. AXF/ELF/OUT is one symbol-source field; MAP is independent. Project initialization remains a backend/AI workflow and is not exposed as duplicate desktop project overview state.

RTT address search fills the editable address field but never starts acquisition automatically. RTT transmit is raw passthrough on the selected channel with Abc/Hex encoding, Enter or button send, bounded history, and explicit no/\r/\n/\r\n endings.

Packaged Tauri builds inject http://127.0.0.1:8765 as the local control-plane origin; development builds keep relative URLs and the Vite proxy. Standard release builds generate NSIS only unless MSI or WebView2-offline output is explicitly requested.

hardware.probe: MKLink V4 connected; identifier intentionally omitted
hardware.online_flash_project: STM32F103 bootloader fixture; local path intentionally omitted
hardware.stream_hil_project: STM32F103 application fixture; local path intentionally omitted
hardware.keil_root: local Keil installation; path intentionally omitted
hardware.permission: User permits adding test firmware code, compiling with Keil, flashing, and testing VOFA/SystemView/variable reads.

known_limits: Physical target power loss, probe unplug, SWD wire disconnect, second non-MKLink probe comparison, and physical network disconnect were not executed.

Only RTT retained a measured overload boundary. SystemView pairs=2/tick and VOFA/SuperWatch at the fastest supported 10 us request are qualified stable zero-drop rates, not claimed drop-bounded maxima. The latest installed two-channel SuperWatch gate sustained 24350.43 samples/s/channel for 600.737 seconds at the 10 us request.
Real hidden-tab HIL remains not established: Edge 130 rejected the visibility override and a background tab did not set document.hidden=true; deterministic hidden scheduling tests pass.
GUI npm audit reports two pre-existing high vulnerabilities; dependencies were not changed during protocol work.
Task 3 uses the first CONTROL heartbeat as a subscription-ready barrier in tests because batches published before subscribe are intentionally not cached.
The Windows RC executables and installers are unsigned and may show an unknown-publisher warning.

The first final-package restricted-environment teardown omitted SystemDrive in the test harness, so Windows created a literal test-cache directory after product processes and ports exited; the cache was removed and the corrected NSIS lifecycle rerun passed completely.

Task 11 did not reflash the whole-chip stream fixture because the target currently uses a bootloader-preserving App layout; RTT performance remains supported by the existing 600-second packaged HIL artifact.
V2/V3 offline deployment is covered by automated tests but was not physically executed in this session; V4.3.4 is the only newly qualified offline hardware model.

Windows computer-use visual automation was unavailable because its native communication pipe was missing; GUI behavior was verified by 249 Vitest tests, the production build, direct installed FastAPI/WebSocket HIL, and a real Edge Worker/Canvas stroke gate. No new Windows screenshot evidence is claimed.

Computer Use was available for most of the ordinary-user session, then its window capture channel twice timed out with FrameArrived errors. It recovered after the rebuilt installer was installed and completed the final installed-app retest. Screenshots remain local test evidence only and were deleted before commit.

After the explicit packaging pause, Computer Use could not reconnect because its native pipe was missing, and the Browser plugin reported no available browser instance. The final dynamic-interval pass therefore used real Microsoft Edge through local Playwright against the production frontend and installed bundled sidecar; the temporary screenshot was deleted and no screenshot evidence is committed.

Installed Online Flash discovery and App-only programming were physically qualified with MicroKeenV4 only. V2 and V3 share the MicroKeen CMSIS-DAP description policy but were not physically available for this pass.
Serial and Modbus physical fixtures were not established in this ordinary-user pass and remain NOT ESTABLISHED.

Immediately after the large PyInstaller/Rust build, Windows displayed one crashrpt.exe 0xc000012d dialog over the installed app. Dismissing it allowed normal operation; it was not attributed to Mklink without further reproduction.

The 2fb5a8a v0.1.0-rc.2 standard NSIS was qualified on the build machine and uses the bundled sidecar, but has not yet been exercised on a second physical clean Windows computer or clean virtual machine. If WebView2 is absent, the standard bootstrapper may require network access.

The builtin algorithm bundle currently covers 1142 exact targets from six license-reviewed STM32 DFPs plus pyOCD builtin targets. DAPLinkUtility resources and third-party Pack algorithms with unclear redistribution terms were studied but not copied; broader vendor coverage requires explicit license review or authorization.

Optional online Pack installation still requires outbound HTTPS access and may require a user-configured proxy in restricted networks. Offline builtin, local Pack import, and custom FLM paths do not depend on catalog downloads after their files are present.

One installed HIL cold start returned a transient CMSIS-DAP enumeration failure while the USB device remained present and no resource owner existed; a normal application restart restored discovery and both subsequent hardware jobs passed.

The final v0.1.0-rc.2 installed pass verified DownBuffer authority and transmit acceptance but did not establish a fresh byte-exact MCU echo because the target parser coalesced per-byte echo records; the earlier 93d83e4 hardware pass remains the byte-exact payload evidence.

AXF/ELF symbol and RTT address diagnosis requires external arm-none-eabi-readelf. The installed restricted-PATH qualification intentionally had no readelf, so AXF diagnosis was unavailable while independent MAP diagnosis and hardware RTT startup passed.
Do not expose full probe IDs, COM ports, credentials, user names, raw logs, screenshots, Pack files, or build artifacts in Git.
next_actions: Install the 2fb5a8a v0.1.0-rc.2 standard NSIS candidate on a second clean Windows 10/11 computer or virtual machine with no Python, Node, Rust, Keil, or pre-existing Pack cache.
